paper.title: Detecting Glutathione and Related Antioxidants as Biomarkers in Patient Breast Tumor Tissues: An Update in the Age of Metabolomics

entities.name: Detecting Glutathione and Related Antioxidants as Biomarkers in Patient Breast Tumor Tissues: An Update in the Age of Metabolomics

evidence_entities.text: Increased levels of glutathione (GSH) and related antioxidant processes are thought to predict breast tumor aggressiveness and therapy response.
In our 2012 review of 21 studies, we found that most patient breast tumors exhibited increased GSH levels compared to peritumoral tissue.
Despite this, these recent studies confirm that the levels of most of the antioxidants or related metabolites are increased in patient breast tumor tissues compared to normal tissues.
In addition, poor patient outcomes are often associated with tumor tissues with higher GSH and lower Tau levels.
GSH levels also increase with histological grade.

properties.direction: higher GSH associated with poor outcomes
lower Tau associated with poor outcomes
